weather_line: Conditional clearing southwest · ink drying · ducks oriented

stories.headline: Christine Reads the Ink Difference Against the Hempworth November Margins, and the Condition Turns Out to Have a Name
body: The second ink on the southwest quadrant of the 'Passable (Conditional)' chart is not a later addition, as initially suspected, but a correction — applied over an erasure so thorough the original notation left only a ghost line. Christine identified this on Friday morning by holding the relevant section at an angle to the kitchen window light, a technique she has used on Gringotts documents twice before and has never publicly named. The ghost line, when traced against the Hempworth November margins, reads as a drainage catchment boundary. The condition is water.

Specifically: the southwest quadrant is passable in July and August, when the upper catchment drains through a secondary channel that only establishes itself after the spring flush. In September it becomes the primary channel again. The cartographer who made the correction knew this. The cartographer who wrote 'Conditional' apparently did not, or chose not to say. Steve, when Christine showed him the window-angle result, looked at it for eleven seconds and then said it was exactly what he had thought, which Christine accepted as fully credible and also as a compliment.

The brass ducks remain oriented southwest. The Jeep is still at its new angle. No departure date has been named, but Steve has measured the kitchen table twice this week, and the table has not changed.

stories.headline: Friday at Gringotts Proceeds Efficiently; Commendation Portrait Passed Seven Times; No One Has Mentioned It Aloud
body: Christine's route through the Accounts Liaison Wing passes the third pillar on the left twice per standard working day: once inbound, once out. On Friday she made the journey three additional times on routine business, bringing the total to seven passes of the framed commendation filed under 'Anomalous (Positive), Precedent: Pending.' The duty goblin, Skreech, has now viewed the frame on twelve separate occasions. Neither Christine nor Skreech has acknowledged the other's viewing.
The Sub-Committee has not formally disbanded. This is understood to be precautionary.

stories.headline: Ministry Releases Friday Summary Describing the Week as 'Concluded'; Stops Short of Characterising It as 'Good'
body: The Department of Magical Coordination issued its end-of-week bulletin at 4:47 p.m. on Friday, confirming that the week had ended and listing four items resolved, two items ongoing, and one item reclassified as 'ambient.' The bulletin did not elaborate on 'ambient.' A Ministry spokesperson, when asked, described it as 'a courtesy designation for matters that have stopped escalating without technically stopping.'

The glowing briefcase, last recorded in Sub-Basement C, has not been logged in or out since Wednesday. The Sub-Basement C ledger shows the entry in handwriting that does not match any current staff member. The Ministry describes this as consistent with standard Friday conditions.

stories.headline: Household Reaches End of Week; Assessments Filed; One Assessment Disputed by Its Own Author Before Submission
body: Friday evening settled over the household in the orderly fashion associated with weeks that have resolved their primary questions and are waiting for the next ones to begin. Steve confirmed the Jeep's angle was intentional. Christine confirmed the kitchen table was the correct size. The brass ducks have not moved since Wednesday, which all parties regard as appropriate given the pace of developments.

Biscuit inventory levels were assessed and found satisfactory by two of three interested parties. The third party (Lucy) filed a minority report contesting the methodology and was overruled on procedural grounds.

familiars_corner: Lucy: The minority report has been submitted to a higher authority — specifically, the area under the kitchen table — where it will be reconsidered at the earliest opportunity, which Lucy estimates as approximately whenever the biscuit tin is unattended.

Ellie: Ellie has discovered that the kitchen window, held at a particular angle, produces a reflection that appears to be a second dog looking in from outside. She has visited four times to check on it and is satisfied that it seems friendly.

Jersey: Jersey has identified Friday as structurally identical to Thursday in all the ways that matter, namely warmth and available laps, and has concluded that the week has been excellent throughout.
founding_editor_note: That window-angle trick works on blueprints too — I used it on the back-bedroom framing in 1987 and I'll tell you the ghost line saved us a week. She learned that somewhere sensible. — D.

in_brief.sport: Weekend weights log remains sealed; the bar has not commented; the bar is understood to be ready.
in_brief.forecast: Clearing conditional by midmorning · secondary channel navigable · departure weather pending confirmation.

storyline_updates.title: Passable (Conditional)
storyline_updates.summary: Day 3 resolved: Christine identified the condition by holding the chart to window light and reading the ghost line against the Hempworth November drainage margins. The condition is water — specifically, the secondary channel that runs July through August only. Steve confirmed this was exactly his working theory, which Christine accepted. The southwest terrain is passable now, in July. No departure date named. Brass ducks still southwest. Jeep at its new angle. Day 4 should deliver the resolution: a departure date is named, the route is committed to, and the southwest quadrant is visited in the only way that settles a chart — by going there.
